assignment_description:
Paper Work Model 1 Wall Pocket
⍞ Materials: Sloyd by Schwartz, tagboard, ruler, pencil, scissors, tape, awl, hole punch, thread, needle (optional)

➜ PREP
Before the term begins, read p.3-9 to get an understanding of and a refresher on the practice and method of sloyd. It is good to be reminded of the importance of the instructional words you use to help your students understand and focus. Reading the introduction will also allow you to become familiar with the terminology in a new book before teaching your student.

➜ VIEW
Photo of the model and tutorial. Visualize the steps to work the model.
∞ Image & Video Link: Wall Pocket

➜ PRACTICE
Work on the model. Read and demonstrate instructions one step at a time.
Paper Work: 3-D Geometric Shapes "No.1: Wall Pocket" p.10
Tack the wall pocket to your bulletin board or message board as a way to organize small things like thumbtacks, paper clips, flower clippings, etc.
